mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates signal weak labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production and inflation signals economic overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL are rising.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate robust demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1200.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates and high inflation signal contractionary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances during tightening cycles despite rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth signals a healthy labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances with strong PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDP and real sales indicate expanding demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where both GDPC1 and RSAFS are rising.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation signals potential monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.
